description: §8.2/§8.3/§8.4 real WHERE-filter SELECT (RUNTIME, sqlEngine:"real") — the seam proof. loadByRole(role) runs `SELECT id, name FROM users WHERE role = ${role} ORDER BY id`.all(); a real Bun.SQL in-memory SQLite (Fork B loose-infer: columns id/name/role inferred from the seed rows) actually EVALUATES the WHERE and binds the ?1 param, returning only the two admin rows (id/name columns). The regex stub ignored WHERE and returned the whole 3-row table with all columns — so this assertion passes ONLY on the real engine, proving the sqlEngine:"real" seam.
rationale: §8.2 bound-param semantics: `${role}` compiles to a positional param the emitted `_scrml_sql`...` binds — the STUB drops `...v` entirely. §8.3 `.all()` = Row[]. The real Bun.SQL engine filters `role = 'admin'` server-side; the stub returned all rows for `FROM users`. Seeds [Alice/admin, Bob/user, Carol/admin]; the filtered `.all()` hydrates @admins with the two admin rows (id-ordered). This is the minimal real-engine dimension the stub structurally cannot express (empirically: the stub yields all 3 rows with all columns). No <schema> is needed — the seed rows loose-infer the columns; constraint-bearing cases carry a <schema> instead.
